judge_reason: Both sides agree on the main visual content (grainy IR/thermal photo with crosshair reticle, dark object above center, black redaction blocks, timestamp at bottom left), but Description B adds specific quoted timestamp text not mentioned in Description A, and Description A notes a disc-shaped object while B calls it 'blurry object' and adds an extra speck, yet these are detail differences rather than clear contradictions on verbatim text.
original_image_tags: Black-and-white infrared (IR) sensor still, likely from an aircraft or targeting pod. A central crosshair reticle with numbered tick marks (5, 10, 15) on both horizontal and vertical axes is visible. A small, dark, disc-shaped or oval object is located in the upper central portion of the frame, just above the vertical reticle line and slightly left of center. The background is a uniform gray (likely sky or haze). Several large, black rectangular blocks obscure data in the corners and along the edges of the image. A timestamp is overlaid in white text at the bottom left.
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: Grainy monochrome surveillance-style photograph of the sky with a central crosshair reticle and numeric tick marks, showing one small dark object near the top center and a second smaller dark speck below center; timestamp “12/31/99 18:10:00” at bottom left.*
